div.p: The ELF p_align (0x1000) field enforces that: p_vaddr % p_align == p_offset % p_align
This allows the kernel to use the same relative offset within a physical page for both the disk file and the RAM page, enabling Demand Paging.
e_ident: \x7F ELF ... e_type: ET_EXEC (2) e_machine: EM_X86_64 (62) e_entry: 0x401000 e_phoff: 64 (0x40) e_shoff: 0 (No sections) e_phnum: 2 e_phentsize: 56
.data (Initialized globals) Kernel allocates extra 0x100 for .bss at runtime
Mapping: File[0x2000:0x2100] Vaddr: 0x403000 - 0x4030FF (.data) Vaddr: 0x403100 - 0x4031FF (.bss) Permissions: RW-
[0] PT_LOAD (Text) p_type: PT_LOAD p_flags: R | X (5) p_offset: 0x1000 p_vaddr: 0x401000 p_filesz: 0x200 p_memsz: 0x200 p_align: 0x1000
[1] PT_LOAD (Data) p_type: PT_LOAD p_flags: R | W (6) p_offset: 0x2000 p_vaddr: 0x403000 p_filesz: 0x100 p_memsz: 0x200 (.bss) p_align: 0x1000
